title: cron field_names/field_ranges parallel constants and indexed lookups are fragile coupling
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
Type Safety / Data Modeling

## Location
src/tools/cron.rs:60-69 (FIELD_NAMES and FIELD_RANGES) and 78-138 (use sites)

## Violation
```rust
const FIELD_NAMES: [&str; 5] = ["minute (0-59)", "hour (0-23)", "day (1-31)", "month (1-12)", "weekday (0-6)"];
const FIELD_RANGES: [(u32, u32); 5] = [(0, 59), (0, 23), (1, 31), (1, 12), (0, 6)];
const _: () = assert!(FIELD_NAMES.len() == FIELD_RANGES.len());
```

The compile-time assert is acknowledgement that the two parallel arrays need to stay in sync — but it only guards the length, not the *meaning* of paired entries. Swapping `(0, 23)` and `(1, 31)` (hour vs day) would compile and pass the assert; cron expressions for hour 30 would then incorrectly validate while hour 12 of "day" field would not.

The 5-element duplication exists because both lookups happen in the same loop with the same index `i` (lines 81-138). One struct per field would carry both pieces of information together.

## Mandated refactor
```rust
struct CronField { name: &'static str, min: u32, max: u32 }
const FIELDS: [CronField; 5] = [
    CronField { name: "minute (0-59)", min: 0, max: 59 },
    CronField { name: "hour (0-23)", min: 0, max: 23 },
    CronField { name: "day (1-31)", min: 1, max: 31 },
    CronField { name: "month (1-12)", min: 1, max: 12 },
    CronField { name: "weekday (0-6)", min: 0, max: 6 },
];
```

(This also disappears entirely when the parser is replaced with the cron crate per the separate issue.)

## Filed from QA review dispatch
